<<Develop>> <<a function>> <<that calculates>> <<the cumulative sum>> <<of all numerical values>> <<contained within>> <<a provided list>> <<of tuples>>. <<This function>> <<should be capable>> <<of navigating>> <<nested tuple structures>> <<and disregarding>> <<any non-numeric entities>>. <<It needs to handle>> <<both integers>> <<and floats>> <<within the tuples>> <<regardless of their depth>> <<in the structure>>. <<The function>> <<must be robust>> <<and efficient>> <<to process>> <<complex data>> <<without errors>>. <<By iterating>> <<through each element>> <<and checking>> <<its type>> <<it can extract>> <<only the numeric values>> <<and sum them up>>.

******  
<<The first approach>> <<involves a recursive function>> <<that traverses>> <<each element>> <<within the tuples>> <<and checks>> <<if it is a tuple or a number>>. <<If it is a tuple>> <<the function calls itself>> <<recursively>> <<to process the inner elements>>. <<If it is a number>> <<it adds it to the total sum>>. <<This method>> <<ensures that all levels>> <<of nesting are covered>> <<and all numeric values>> <<are accounted for>>. <<It also ignores>> <<any strings or other data types>> <<that may be present>> <<in the tuples>>. <<By using recursion>> <<the function can handle>> <<even deeply nested structures>> <<with ease>>.

******  
<<The second approach>> <<utilizes a stack-based method>> <<to simulate recursion>> <<without explicitly using recursive calls>>. <<This method>> <<pushes elements>> <<onto a stack>> <<and processes them one by one>> <<until the stack is empty>>. <<Each time a tuple is encountered>> <<it is expanded>> <<and its elements are added to the stack>>. <<Numbers are directly added>> <<to the sum>>. <<This approach>> <<can be more efficient>> <<in certain scenarios>> <<and avoids potential stack overflow issues>> <<that may arise with deep recursion>>. <<It also maintains the same level of accuracy>> <<and completeness>> <<as the recursive method>>.

******  
<<Both approaches>> <<require the function to check the type of each element>> <<before performing any operations>>. <<This ensures>> <<that only numeric values>> <<are included in the sum>>.
